title: assertpmux pass generates wrong assertions for priority case
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
test.sv:
```
module test(input logic clk, output logic out1, out2, input logic [1:0] foo);

	assume property (foo == 3);

	always_ff @(posedge clk) begin
		priority case (1)
			foo == 3: out1 = 0;
			foo[0]:   out1 = 1;
			default;
			// should NOT assert, because first case (foo==3) matched and also because default case exists
		endcase

		priority case (foo)
			2: out2 = 0;
			1: out2 = 1;
			// should assert, because case foo==3 doesn't exist
		endcase
	end

endmodule
```

test.sby:
```
[tasks]
test

[options]
mode bmc
append 10
expect pass
multiclock on
depth 20

[engines]
smtbmc

[script]
read_verilog -sv -formal test.sv
prep -top test
assertpmux

[files]
test.sv
```
Or download:
[test.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/6018876/test.zip)

Run:
`sby test.sby`

## Expected behavior

The assertion for the first priority case should not trigger, because foo is 3 and that case exists. Also there is a default case, which in my understanding should never cause an assertion to trigger.

The assertion for the second priority case should trigger, because there is no case for foo==3 and the default case doesn't exist.

## Actual behavior

The opposite happens: The assertion for the first priority case triggers and the one for the second does not.
code_blocks:
[tasks]
test

[options]
mode bmc
append 10
expect pass
multiclock on
depth 20

[engines]
smtbmc

[script]

prep -top test

[files]
test.sv
